_purpose: Example call to amplify_suggest_pattern_key — find the canonical pattern_key for a recurring lesson before recording it. Prevents two sessions from inventing two different keys for the same lesson and splitting the frequency counter.

input.title: Read Hermes API spec before integration
input.description: Stop guessing endpoint parameters, check the documentation first.

_typical_output_when_match_exists:
Existing pattern_keys for project "my-api" similar to "Read Hermes API spec before integration":

  • "read-docs-before-coding" — similarity 0.412, frequency 3
      example: Read NIM API docs first

Pick one of these if it actually describes the same recurring lesson. If none do, coin a new key (e.g. "read-hermes-api-spec-before-integration") rather than forcing a partial match.
_typical_output_when_no_match:
No existing pattern_key for project "my-api" scored above 0.3.
Suggested NEW key: "read-hermes-api-spec-before"

Use this key when calling amplify_learn, or pick your own. Reusing the same key across recurring lessons is what makes the frequency counter actually count.
_known_limitation: Trigram Jaccard catches shared keywords and morphology (read/reading, docs/documentation) but does not catch pure synonyms. verify vs confirm score far apart even when the meaning is identical. Document your pattern_key choices so the next session knows the canonical form.
_when_to_use: Call this before amplify_learn when you suspect the lesson is a recurring pattern, not a one-off. Especially useful when picking up a project after a break.
